name: United States

colors.symbolism: hardiness and valour (per Charles Thomson's 1782 explanation of the related U.S. seal)

description: Thirteen equal horizontal stripes alternating red (top and bottom) and white. In the upper hoist, a navy-blue canton bearing fifty white five-pointed stars arranged in nine offset horizontal rows of six and five stars (6-5-6-5-6-5-6-5-6).
history: The basic stars-and-stripes design was prescribed by the Flag Resolution of the Second Continental Congress on 14 June 1777: thirteen alternating red and white stripes and thirteen white stars on a blue field. The Flag Act of 4 April 1818 fixed the stripes at thirteen and provided that a star be added for each new state on the 4th of July following admission. The current 50-star design was ordered by President Eisenhower on 21 August 1959 after Hawaii's admission and became official on 4 July 1960; it is the longest-used version of the U.S. flag. Designed by Robert G. Heft as a high-school project; selected from over 1,500 submissions.

features.symbolism: the thirteen original colonies that declared independence from Great Britain in 1776
the fifty states of the Union; a new constellation
